title: '`podman`' '`completion`' subcommand not part of the '`podman -h`' output
body:
_podman v. 5.8.0_

Hello. While the '`podman`' '`completion`' subcommand exists
```
$ podman completion -h | head -n 1
Generate shell autocompletions
```
it is not part of the '`podman -h`' output.

```
$ podman -h | awk '$1=$1' | \
sed -n '/^Available Commands/,/^Options/{/^Available Commands/!{/^Options/!p}}' | \
awk '{print $1}' | column
artifact	help		mount		secret
attach		history		network		start
auto-update	image		pause		stats
build		images		pod			stop
commit		import		port		system
compose		info		ps			tag
container	init		pull		top
cp			inspect		push		unmount
create		kill		quadlet		unpause
diff		kube		rename		unshare
events		load		restart		untag
exec		login		rm			update
export		logout		rmi			version
farm		logs		run			volume
generate	machine		save		wait
healthcheck	manifest	search
```
Perhaps as a consequence, and perhaps as intended, it is not part of the available command completions in Bash Shell.

'`podman`  <kbd>tab.</kbd> + <kbd>tab.</kbd>'

comments.body: The completion is explicitly marked `Hidden: true` in `cmd/podman/completion/completion.go` with the comment `// don’t show this command to users`.
